stories.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
stories.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

stories.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
stories.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

stories.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
stories.description: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

stories.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
stories.description: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.

stories.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
stories.description: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.description: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

stories.title: 'Growing sense in international community' that Trump doesn't have an Iran exit strategy
stories.description: Amid fresh transatlantic tensions surrounding Iran, NATO, and US troops in Germany, Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Dr Christopher Featherstone, Associate Lecturer in Politics and International Relations at the University of York. Dr Featherstone sees a strained alliance cau...

stories.title: Beirut-based director Lana Daher on her debut film 'Do You Love Me'
stories.description: A Beirut-based filmmaker whose debut feature film has been described as an ode to the collective courage of the Lebanese people has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the film and her love for the city. Lana Daher has spent years working in Beirut and the last six years delving through th...

stories.title: Energy fears drive Gulf mediation in US-Iran conflict
stories.description: Gulf countries are quietly pressuring the United States and Iran to reach a deal as the conflict disrupts vital oil exports and takes a growing economic toll. Working behind the scenes, including through Pakistan, they are trying to revive negotiations despite tensions with Tehra...

stories.title: Viral pro-Iran AI memes blur lines between propaganda and entertainment
stories.description: Iranian-American creator Mahdi Hemmat is gaining millions of views with AI-generated, Lego-style rap videos that blend satire, pop culture and political messaging about the conflict involving Iran, the United States and Israel. While presented as entertainment, the content promot...

stories.title: Mali crisis deepens as separatists and jihadists step up attacks
stories.description: Mali’s military launched strikes on Kidal after separatist Azawad Liberation Front fighters claimed control, while coordinated attacks with jihadist group JNIM intensified insecurity. As rebels push to expand their reach and demand the withdrawal of Russian forces, jihadists have...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade: 'Who will blink first' ?
stories.description: Donald Trump has discussed with oil companies how to cushion the impact of a months-long US blockade of Iranian ports, as reports of potential new strikes pushed Brent crude to its highest level since March 2022. For FRANCE 24's international editor Angela Diffley, both sides are...

stories.title: US military equipment worth billions of dollars destroyed in Iran war
stories.description: The United States lost aerial equipment worth up to $2.8bn, according to a US-based think tank.

stories.title: Was Israeli PM’s Lebanon destruction video a snub to Trump?
stories.description: Hours after Trump asked Netanyahu to stop destroying buildings in Lebanon, Israel's leader posted a destruction montage.

stories.title: Why are Western feminists silent on the war on Iranian women?
stories.description: Those who backed Iranian women’s protests against compulsory hijab are silent as those same women are killed.

stories.title: $25bn or $1 trillion: How much has Iran war really cost the US?
stories.description: Heated exchange on Capitol Hill reflects growing unease among lawmakers over a lack of transparency in cost of Iran war.

stories.title: Israel kills nine people in southern Lebanon despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.description: Israeli attacks and threats on southern Lebanon escalate, as Lebanese government calls out Israel's ceasefire violations

stories.title: Russia has given Iran something more important than weapons
stories.description: Russia's political backing and objection to escalation have proved much more consequential in shaping the conflict.

stories.title: Tracking the shadow fleet: How Iran evaded the US naval blockade in Hormuz
stories.description: Iran's shadow fleet uses fake flags and dark ships to evade the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Will the Iran war reshape the global energy order?
stories.description: As oil markets shift, OPEC's grip is under pressure, US exports rise, and China drives the push towards renewables.

stories.title: Amazon says damaged UAE cloud region recovery to take several months
stories.description: Amazon said on Thursday that restoring cloud computing operations in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), which were damaged in the conflict in the Middle East, is expected to take several months. Amazon’s data centres in the region were hit by Iranian retaliatory drone strikes in ear...

stories.title: In fresh message, Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei defies US naval blockade
stories.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei declared on Thursday that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from President Donald Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come. “Today, tw...

stories.title: Mamdani calls on King Charles to return Koh-i-Noor diamond
stories.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called for King Charles to “return” the prized Koh-i-Noor diamond, which the British Empire took from the Indian subcontinent in the 1800s, on the third day of the monarch’s state visit on Wednesday. Before greeting Charles and Queen Camilla at ...

stories.title: US turns down Iranian proposal to end war
stories.description: • Trump warns Iran to ‘get smart soon’, accept N-curbs; signals months-long extension of naval blockade• Tehran warns of ‘unprecedented military action’ over shipping curbs• USS Gerald R. Ford to return after 10-month deployment in ME TEHRAN/WASHIN­GTON: Amid reports about Iran’s...

stories.title: Pentagon chief spars with lawmakers over Iran war
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth repeatedly clashed with Democratic lawmakers over the Iran conflict on Wednesday, in his first testimony to Congress since President Donald Trump launched the war more than two months ago. Appearing before the House Armed Services Com...

stories.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in 'terrorist' attack in London
stories.description: Two Jewish men were stabbed on Wednesday in north London, an attack police declared a “terrorist incident” and which follows a series of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites. King Charles III, Prime Minister Keir Starmer and London mayor Sadiq Khan led the condemnation of the “ap...

stories.title: Mojtaba Khamenei says Iran will protect its 'nuclear, missile capabilities'; U.S. suffered ‘disgraceful defeat’
stories.description: The supreme leader said the only place Americans belong in the Persian Gulf is “at the bottom of its waters,” as the Strait of Hormuz remains in Tehran's chokehold

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Strait of Hormuz will be 'free from American presence', Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says
stories.description: Pakistan said that it was actively engaged with the U.S. and Iran to end the war in West Asia

stories.title: U.S. seeks international help to reopen Strait of Hormuz as crude prices surge
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is slated to receive a briefing on April 30 on plans for a series of fresh military ‌strikes on Iran in hopes of making Iran more flexible on nuclear issues in negotiations

stories.title: Iran offers to end chokehold on Strait of Hormuz, asks U.S. to end blockade
stories.description: Iran also wants the U.S. to end its blockade of the country as part of its proposal, said the two officials, who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the closed-door negotiations

stories.title: Oil prices ease after rally as UAE exits OPEC; Iran conflict, Hormuz blockade keep supply concerns high
stories.description: Brent crude futures for June dipped 1% to $111.25 a barrel, having climbed for the previous seven sessions

stories.title: Trump warns Iran better 'get smart soon' and accept nuclear deal
stories.description: U.S. could extend its naval blockade of Iran for months more, oil executives were told in a meeting with Mr. Trump, an official said, after press reports that he had rejected Iran's latest proposed deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Russian deputy foreign minister discusses Middle East goings-on with Omani officials
stories.description: "Key international and regional issues were the focus of significant attention, including the situations in Iran, the Persian Gulf, Yemen, Lebanon, and the Palestinian-Israeli conflict," the Russian Foreign Ministry said

stories.title: China not supplying air defense systems, drones to Iran — defense ministry
stories.description: Chinese Defense Ministry Spokesman Zhang Xiaogang clarified that China had friendly military ties with Iraq

stories.title: China does not benefit from Iran's conflict with US — Defense Ministry
stories.description: Chinese Defense Ministry representative Zhang Xiaogang emphasized that Beijing had always sought to promote a ceasefire and the end of hostilities

stories.title: Germany facing growing shortage of feedstock, components due to conflict around Iran
stories.description: The deficit is primarily experienced in industries with a strong dependence on energy-intensive and petroleum products, Ifo Institute for Economic Research said
